diagnostics.message: `saturate(saturate(x))` is redundant — the inner `saturate` already clamps to [0, 1]; drop the outer call
`clamp(x, 0, 1)` is `saturate(x)` — saturate folds into a free output modifier on AMD/NVIDIA/Intel; clamp lowers to a min/max pair
`clamp(x, 0, 1)` is `saturate(x)` — saturate folds into a free output modifier on AMD/NVIDIA/Intel; clamp lowers to a min/max pair
`normalize(normalize(x))` is redundant -- the inner `normalize` already produces a unit-length vector; drop the outer call
`transpose(transpose(M))` is redundant -- transpose is its own inverse; the two calls cancel
`abs(...)` is redundant here -- a self-product `x * x` is always non-negative, so the wrapping `abs` adds an instruction with no effect
`abs(...)` is redundant here -- `dot(v, v)` is the squared magnitude and is always non-negative, so the wrapping `abs` adds an instruction with no effect
`abs(...)` is redundant here -- `saturate` already clamps its result to [0, 1], so the wrapping `abs` adds an instruction with no effect
